QF.id: VII Market Participants in Securities Markets_20

question.text: An 'insider' under SEBI (Prohibition of Insider Trading) Regulations, 2015 is defined as:

answer.text: A connected person OR any person in possession of or having access to unpublished price sensitive information
Only a director or officer of a listed company
Only a person who has traded based on UPSI
An employee of SEBI who monitors market activity

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Regulation 2(g) defines an insider as: (i) a connected person (as per Regulation 2(d)), OR (ii) any person in possession of or having access to UPSI — regardless of how they obtained it. The definition casts a very wide net. **The Trap:** You don't need to be a company employee or director to be an insider. ANY person with access to UPSI is an insider, regardless of their designation or how they obtained the information. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series XII and Series III-A (Compliance) cover insider trading regulations in detail. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The insider definition is deliberately broad — anyone with UPSI is an insider, period. The onus is on the person who traded to demonstrate they were NOT in possession of UPSI or their trading was covered by exonerating circumstances.
